title: g-tensor Optimization in Ge/SiGe Quantum Dots
authors: Aram Shojaei, Edmondo Valvo, Maximilian Rimbach-Russ, Eliska Greplova, Ana Silva
abstract: Planar germanium heterostructures hosting hole-spin qubits are among the leading platforms for scalable semiconductor-based quantum computing. Yet, device performance is hindered by significant quantum dot variability, which leads to uncertainty in qubit energy levels and random orientations of the spin quantization axis. Tailored control of the g-tensor offers a strategy to overcome these limitations and achieve more reliable qubit operations. Here, we introduce a flexible optimization framework for engineering g-tensor properties. As a benchmark, we numerically obtain the optimal reshaping of the out-of-plane potential in a SiGe-Ge-SiGe quantum well to suppress the in-plane g-tensor components and realize the recently proposed gapless single-spin qubit encoding. This reshaping is achieved through heterostructure engineering, specifically by adjusting the silicon concentration within the quantum well, though the framework remains readily adaptable to alternative design objectives. Our results provide practical design principles for improving the tunability of the spin response, paving the way towards large-scale germanium-based quantum computers.
summary: This paper presents a numerical framework for optimizing the g-tensor in germanium-based quantum dots to mitigate the effects of device variability. By engineering the heterostructure potential, the authors show it is possible to suppress specific g-tensor components, which is a key step toward reliable, large-scale semiconductor quantum computing.
main_problem: Device performance in planar germanium heterostructures is limited by quantum dot variability, which causes uncertainty in qubit energy levels and random spin quantization axis orientations.
main_result: The authors developed a flexible optimization framework to engineer g-tensor properties, demonstrating that reshaping the out-of-plane potential in SiGe-Ge-SiGe quantum wells can suppress in-plane g-tensor components to enable gapless single-spin qubit encoding.
method: A numerical optimization framework is used to design heterostructure engineering parameters, specifically adjusting silicon concentration within the quantum well to reshape the potential.
model_or_system: The system consists of planar germanium heterostructures (SiGe-Ge-SiGe quantum wells) hosting hole-spin qubits.
key_observables: g-tensor components (in-plane and out-of-plane), spin quantization axis orientation, and qubit energy levels.
important_parameters: Silicon concentration within the quantum well, out-of-plane potential profile, and g-tensor anisotropy.
main_assumptions: The framework assumes that heterostructure engineering via silicon concentration adjustment is a viable way to reshape the potential.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of variability in Ge-based qubits, presents a new optimization framework for g-tensor engineering, uses the reshaping of SiGe-Ge-SiGe quantum wells as a benchmark case, and concludes with implications for scalable quantum computing.
analysis_basis: abstract only; PDF text extraction failed or PDF unavailable
